questions.question: What is the largest continent by area?
questions.explanation: Asia covers about 44.6 million km², nearly 30% of Earth's land area.

questions.question: What is the longest river in the world?
questions.explanation: The Nile stretches approximately 6,650 km through northeastern Africa.

questions.question: What is the capital of Japan?
questions.explanation: Tokyo has been Japan's capital since 1868.

questions.question: What is the smallest country in the world by area?
questions.explanation: Vatican City covers just 0.44 km² (110 acres).

questions.question: What strait separates Europe from Africa?
questions.explanation: The Strait of Gibraltar is only 14.3 km wide at its narrowest point.

questions.question: Mount Kilimanjaro is located in which country?
questions.explanation: Kilimanjaro is in northeastern Tanzania, near the Kenyan border.

questions.question: What is the deepest point in the ocean?
questions.explanation: Challenger Deep in the Mariana Trench reaches about 10,935 meters.

questions.question: What country has the most time zones?
questions.explanation: France has 12 time zones due to its overseas territories.

questions.question: What is the driest inhabited continent?
questions.explanation: Australia receives an average of only 470mm of rain per year.

questions.question: What ocean is the largest?
questions.explanation: The Pacific Ocean covers about 165.25 million km².
